title: Log http roundtrips in trace level for docker_client
body:
### Feature request description

When working in an enterprise environment you often have proxies to setup and not being able to see podman docker_client (registry v2/v1) http requests does not help to validate a setup is right/wrong.

### Suggest potential solution

Adding in docker_client a roundtrip implementation logging the http requests/responses would help (important parts: headers mainly in my case but payloads would be nice)

### Have you considered any alternatives?

* Recompiling podman but not sane on the long run
* Using another client (but sticking to redhat distro tools is desired)

comments.body: I think @mtrmac has a PR to trace http requests etc.  Is that something we could use?

There’s https://github.com/containers/image/pull/201 . It is missing documentation, and it logs everything including credentials (which is not an _obvious_ consequence of `--log-level=trace`, so I’m not quite happy about that part).

Having that functionality would definitely be helpful a few times a year — but so far I couldn’t prioritize getting it to a state I’d be really happy with. If anyone has spare cycles, I’m perfectly fine with handing that over.

If it helps a lot of servers decided to either log raw data (literally sending bytes to a string buffer/logger) or (my preference) use a curl format. So a CurlRoundTripper would be welcomed. If you want you can just align it on k8s and reuse their impl maybe: https://github.com/kubernetes/client-go/blob/master/transport/round_trippers.go#L72 (the expected magic https://github.com/kubernetes/client-go/blob/master/transport/round_trippers.go#L367).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
